description: Balanced local-knowledge integration across participation diversity influence trust protection reciprocity accountability and implementation burden.
Prioritizes multiple knowledge systems including scientific local Indigenous practitioner and lived knowledge.
Prioritizes whether local knowledge actually changes decisions resources rules and plans.
Prioritizes legitimacy relationship repair trusted institutions and follow-through.
Prioritizes consent privacy Indigenous data sovereignty and protection against knowledge extraction or harm.
Prioritizes community benefit shared products compensation and return of useful knowledge.
Prioritizes implementation tracking public reporting decision-change logs and corrective action.
Penalizes implementation burden when administrative finance legal or political capacity is constrained.
